activationPolicy: Registry presence never implies skill activation.

skills.name: riss-governance
skills.description: User-facing opt-in governance entrypoint for scoped serious-project routing, source-of-truth checks, narrow agent/tool selection, safety gates, and completion verification.
triggerCases: User explicitly says Use riss-governance in any repository or project thread.
Task involves AI Toolkit, RISS, Supabase/backend/security/release governance, source-of-truth checks, custom agent routing, or completion verification.
Plain-language request needs professional engineering routing inside the primary RISS/AI Toolkit/VD governance context.
negativeTriggerCases: Unrelated projects where user did not explicitly invoke Use riss-governance.
Simple one-off questions where repo governance does not materially help.
Requests to install or activate every tool without review.
Vague quality language in unrelated repositories without explicit governance opt-in.
conflicts: Do not duplicate Superpowers or Codex plugins.
Do not silently fall back from native agent to compiled instructions for high-risk work.
Do not treat explicit opt-in as approval for writes, migrations, package changes, Supabase policy/auth/billing/deployment changes, global config changes, external installs, or broad plugin use.
Do not represent selected agents as spawned agents unless they actually ran.
skills.publicNamingNotes: Future public name is reserved only; current name remains active until alias/wrapper migration is implemented and verified.

skills.name: riss-agent-governance
skills.description: Internal helper skill for agent inventory, native visibility, compiled fallback status, routing, scorecards, handoff governance, and no-silent-fallback checks.
triggerCases: riss-governance internally needs agent selection or fallback status.
riss-governance needs to decide whether extra agents materially improve context, safety, validation, or quality.
negativeTriggerCases: Direct user-facing entrypoint use.
Low-risk typo, wording, or metadata edits where one reviewer-style pass is enough.
Requests to add new core agents or regenerate compiled agents.
conflicts: Must not become a large user-facing governance skill.
Must not add new agents.
Must redirect direct user calls back to riss-governance.

skills.name: riss-skill-governance
skills.description: Internal helper skill for skill inventory, trigger conditions, conflict resolution, missing-capability discovery, skill safety, and candidate preflight.
triggerCases: riss-governance internally needs skill selection, trigger checks, or missing capability discovery.
An extra skill may materially improve context, safety, validation, or quality.
negativeTriggerCases: Direct user-facing entrypoint use.
Low-risk typo, wording, or metadata edits where repo inspection is enough.
Installing, activating, cloning, or copying external skills.
conflicts: Must not install or activate external skills.
Must not copy raw external SKILL.md content.
Must redirect direct user calls back to riss-governance.

skills.name: vd-premium-uiux
skills.description: Direct UI/UX execution skill for premium frontend visual quality, dashboard UX, responsive layout, visual QA, screenshots/browser verification, accessibility, and real estate platform UI.
triggerCases: User asks for premium UI polish.
Accessibility and UI state review request.
Luxury SaaS / real estate interface quality request.
negativeTriggerCases: Backend-only work.
RLS/database-only work.
Security-only review.
Release-only review.
Docs-only change.
Package/dependency change.
Unrelated product strategy.
conflicts: Must not replace riss-governance for safety/release/backend/security governance.
Must not install packages.
Must not claim browser QA passed without rendered evidence.
Must not copy raw external design/skill content.
skills.publicNamingNotes: Future public name is reserved only; current name remains active until alias/wrapper migration is implemented and verified.

skills.name: riss-code-quality
skills.description: React/TypeScript quality, hooks correctness, typed linting, tests, builds, AI-generated code risk, and safe quality-gate routing.
triggerCases: Hooks, lint, tests, build, or AI-generated code risk is in scope.
negativeTriggerCases: Security-only review.
Release-only review.
Requests to add packages or change package manager.
conflicts: Must not install packages.
Must not reformat broadly.
Must not change architecture solely for scanner output.
skills.publicNamingNotes: Future public name is reserved only; current name remains active until alias/wrapper migration is implemented and verified.

skills.name: riss-security-review
skills.description: Tenant isolation, auth, RLS, secrets, public/private payloads, supply-chain, source safety, and security review routing.
triggerCases: Security, privacy, tenant, public payload, auth, RLS, secrets, or supply-chain risk is in scope.
negativeTriggerCases: Pure UI polish with no data/security impact.
Release-only work with no security question.
conflicts: Must not weaken auth or RLS.
Must not read secrets unnecessarily.
Must not run approval-required tools without approval.
skills.publicNamingNotes: Future public name is reserved only; current name remains active until alias/wrapper migration is implemented and verified.

skills.name: riss-release-gate
skills.description: PR readiness, branch hygiene, GitHub checks, CodeRabbit/reviewdog triage, release gates, and post-merge readiness.
triggerCases: PR, CI, checks, CodeRabbit, reviewdog, publish, release, merge, or post-merge readiness is in scope.
negativeTriggerCases: Primary code implementation without release posture.
Requests to merge with pending blockers.
conflicts: Must not push to main.
Must not merge with pending required checks.
Must not mark release ready without evidence.
